title: WeatherBHN — Contract Ledger
description: Master contract performance ledger. One row per settled/active Kalshi weather contract. Source: weather_gold_contract_ledger, populated by refresh_contract_ledger() after each settlement reconciliation run.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total FROM weather_gold_contract_ledger
SELECT COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE contract_resolved_yes IS NOT NULL) AS settled FROM weather_gold_contract_ledger

panels.title: BHN Accuracy (BET_YES + BET_NO)
panels.description: % of BET_YES/BET_NO recommendations that were correct among settled contracts

targets.rawSql: SELECT ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE bhn_correct) / NULLIF(COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE bhn_correct IS NOT NULL), 0), 1) AS accuracy_pct FROM weather_gold_contract_ledger WHERE recommended_action IN ('BET_YES', 'BET_NO')

panels.title: Calibration Accuracy (all contracts)
panels.description: % where calibrated_prob > 0.5 matched the settlement outcome (no action required)

targets.rawSql: SELECT ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE bhn_predicted_correctly) / NULLIF(COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE bhn_predicted_correctly IS NOT NULL), 0), 1) AS calib_accuracy_pct FROM weather_gold_contract_ledger
SELECT COALESCE(ROUND(SUM(paper_pnl), 2), 0) AS total_paper_pnl FROM weather_gold_contract_ledger
SELECT ROUND(AVG(paper_pnl), 2) AS avg_pnl_per_bet FROM weather_gold_contract_ledger WHERE recommended_action IN ('BET_YES', 'BET_NO') AND paper_pnl IS NOT NULL

panels.title: Contract Ledger — All contracts
panels.description: One row per Kalshi contract. Settled = has actual_tmax_f. Sort by target_date desc, then edge desc.

targets.rawSql: SELECT city, target_date, bucket_label, ROUND(calibrated_prob * 100, 1) AS model_pct, ROUND(market_implied_prob * 100, 1) AS market_pct, ROUND(edge_pct, 1) AS edge_pct, recommended_action, signal_strength, model_confidence, stake_usd, actual_tmax_f, CASE WHEN contract_resolved_yes IS NULL THEN 'pending' WHEN contract_resolved_yes THEN 'YES' ELSE 'NO' END AS outcome, CASE WHEN bhn_correct IS NULL THEN '-' WHEN bhn_correct THEN 'correct' ELSE 'wrong' END AS result, ROUND(paper_pnl, 2) AS paper_pnl, CASE WHEN afternoon_storm_flag THEN 'storm' ELSE NULL END AS storm_flag, CASE WHEN sea_breeze_flag THEN 'sea_breeze' ELSE NULL END AS sea_breeze, enso_phase, signal_generated_at FROM weather_gold_contract_ledger ORDER BY target_date DESC, city, bucket_floor

SELECT target_date AS time, SUM(SUM(COALESCE(paper_pnl, 0))) OVER (ORDER BY target_date) AS cumulative_pnl FROM weather_gold_contract_ledger WHERE recommended_action IN ('BET_YES', 'BET_NO') GROUP BY target_date ORDER BY target_date

SELECT target_date, city, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE recommended_action IN ('BET_YES', 'BET_NO') AND paper_pnl IS NOT NULL) AS bets, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE bhn_correct) AS correct, ROUND(SUM(paper_pnl), 2) AS daily_pnl FROM weather_gold_contract_ledger GROUP BY target_date, city ORDER BY target_date DESC, city

panels.title: Accuracy by Model Confidence
panels.description: HIGH = ensemble_spread ≤ 2°F or NWS-GFS delta < 2°F. Does higher confidence = higher accuracy?

targets.rawSql: SELECT model_confidence, ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE bhn_correct) / NULLIF(COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE bhn_correct IS NOT NULL), 0), 1) AS accuracy_pct, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE bhn_correct IS NOT NULL) AS n_bets FROM weather_gold_contract_ledger WHERE recommended_action IN ('BET_YES', 'BET_NO') GROUP BY model_confidence ORDER BY model_confidence

panels.title: Accuracy by Signal Strength
panels.description: STRONG = |edge| ≥ 15%, MODERATE ≥ 8%, WEAK < 8%. Validates that larger edge = more accurate.

targets.rawSql: SELECT signal_strength, ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE bhn_correct) / NULLIF(COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE bhn_correct IS NOT NULL), 0), 1) AS accuracy_pct, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE bhn_correct IS NOT NULL) AS n_bets, ROUND(AVG(paper_pnl), 2) AS avg_pnl FROM weather_gold_contract_ledger WHERE recommended_action IN ('BET_YES', 'BET_NO') AND bhn_correct IS NOT NULL GROUP BY signal_strength ORDER BY signal_strength

panels.title: Accuracy by ENSO Phase
panels.description: Does El Niño / La Niña systematically affect forecast accuracy for KDEN or KMIA?

targets.rawSql: SELECT COALESCE(enso_phase, 'unknown') AS enso_phase, ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE bhn_correct) / NULLIF(COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE bhn_correct IS NOT NULL), 0), 1) AS accuracy_pct, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE bhn_correct IS NOT NULL) AS n_bets FROM weather_gold_contract_ledger WHERE recommended_action IN ('BET_YES', 'BET_NO') GROUP BY enso_phase ORDER BY enso_phase

panels.title: Afternoon Storm Flag vs Accuracy (KMIA)
panels.description: When NWS shows >20% PoP in hours 12-17. Afternoon convection suppresses the daily high ~2-4°F — does the model capture this?

targets.rawSql: SELECT CASE WHEN afternoon_storm_flag THEN 'storm flagged' ELSE 'no storm' END AS storm_flag, ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE bhn_correct) / NULLIF(COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE bhn_correct IS NOT NULL), 0), 1) AS accuracy_pct, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE bhn_correct IS NOT NULL) AS n_bets FROM weather_gold_contract_ledger WHERE station_code = 'KMIA' AND recommended_action IN ('BET_YES', 'BET_NO') AND afternoon_storm_flag IS NOT NULL GROUP BY afternoon_storm_flag ORDER BY afternoon_storm_flag

panels.title: Sea Breeze Flag vs Accuracy (KMIA)
panels.description: When wind is SE/S at >5mph during peak heating. Onshore flow limits afternoon heating ~1.5°F.

targets.rawSql: SELECT CASE WHEN sea_breeze_flag THEN 'sea breeze' ELSE 'no sea breeze' END AS sea_breeze, ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE bhn_correct) / NULLIF(COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE bhn_correct IS NOT NULL), 0), 1) AS accuracy_pct, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE bhn_correct IS NOT NULL) AS n_bets FROM weather_gold_contract_ledger WHERE station_code = 'KMIA' AND recommended_action IN ('BET_YES', 'BET_NO') AND sea_breeze_flag IS NOT NULL GROUP BY sea_breeze_flag ORDER BY sea_breeze_flag

panels.title: Pre-Peak Storm Flag vs Accuracy (KMIA)
panels.description: Storm in [12, peak_hour) — occurs before peak heating, stronger suppression than post-peak.

targets.rawSql: SELECT CASE WHEN pre_peak_storm_flag THEN 'pre-peak storm' ELSE 'no pre-peak storm' END AS pre_peak_flag, ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE bhn_correct) / NULLIF(COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE bhn_correct IS NOT NULL), 0), 1) AS accuracy_pct, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE bhn_correct IS NOT NULL) AS n_bets FROM weather_gold_contract_ledger WHERE station_code = 'KMIA' AND recommended_action IN ('BET_YES', 'BET_NO') AND pre_peak_storm_flag IS NOT NULL GROUP BY pre_peak_storm_flag ORDER BY pre_peak_storm_flag

panels.title: Accuracy by Edge Bucket — Is bigger edge = more accurate?
panels.description: Edge bucketed into 5% bands. Well-calibrated model: accuracy should rise with edge magnitude.

targets.rawSql: SELECT CONCAT(CAST(FLOOR(ABS(edge_pct) / 5) * 5 AS INT), '-', CAST(FLOOR(ABS(edge_pct) / 5) * 5 + 5 AS INT), '%') AS edge_band, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE bhn_correct IS NOT NULL) AS n_bets, ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE bhn_correct) / NULLIF(COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE bhn_correct IS NOT NULL), 0), 1) AS accuracy_pct, ROUND(AVG(paper_pnl), 2) AS avg_pnl FROM weather_gold_contract_ledger WHERE recommended_action IN ('BET_YES', 'BET_NO') AND edge_pct IS NOT NULL GROUP BY FLOOR(ABS(edge_pct) / 5) ORDER BY FLOOR(ABS(edge_pct) / 5)

panels.title: Best and Worst Temperature Buckets by Avg P&L
panels.description: Which bucket ranges does the model most and least reliably predict? Min 3 settled bets.

targets.rawSql: SELECT city, bucket_label, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE paper_pnl IS NOT NULL) AS settled_bets, ROUND(SUM(paper_pnl), 2) AS total_pnl, ROUND(AVG(paper_pnl), 2) AS avg_pnl, ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE bhn_correct) / NULLIF(COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE bhn_correct IS NOT NULL), 0), 1) AS accuracy_pct FROM weather_gold_contract_ledger WHERE recommended_action IN ('BET_YES', 'BET_NO') AND paper_pnl IS NOT NULL GROUP BY city, bucket_label HAVING COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE paper_pnl IS NOT NULL) >= 3 ORDER BY avg_pnl DESC

panels.title: NWS vs GFS MAE by City (last 90 days)
panels.description: Mean absolute error per forecast source. Lower = better. MAE drives sigma for the Gaussian model and will weight the ensemble once Brier-score history is sufficient.

targets.rawSql: SELECT source_name, city, COUNT(*) AS sample_size, ROUND(AVG(ABS(forecast_error_f)), 2) AS mae_f, ROUND(AVG(forecast_error_f), 2) AS avg_bias_f, ROUND(STDDEV(forecast_error_f), 2) AS sigma_f FROM weather_silver_forecast_error WHERE target_date > CURRENT_DATE - 90 GROUP BY source_name, city ORDER BY city, mae_f

panels.title: NWS vs NBM vs GFS Probability Comparison (recent)
panels.description: Per settled contract: NWS Gaussian prob, NBM piecewise CDF prob, GFS ensemble member prob, and the blended raw_model_prob. Where do they diverge?

targets.rawSql: SELECT city, target_date, bucket_label, ROUND(nws_high_prob_pct * 100, 1) AS nws_nbm_pct, ROUND(gfs_high_prob_pct * 100, 1) AS gfs_pct, ROUND(raw_model_prob * 100, 1) AS blended_pct, ROUND(market_implied_prob * 100, 1) AS market_pct, CASE WHEN contract_resolved_yes THEN 'YES' WHEN contract_resolved_yes = FALSE THEN 'NO' ELSE 'pending' END AS outcome FROM weather_gold_contract_ledger WHERE contract_resolved_yes IS NOT NULL ORDER BY target_date DESC LIMIT 40
